user_query:
Sweetie, would you help Nana set up a tiny, reproducible local deployment that builds a Yarn Quality Dashboard from my files without pulling in anything from the Internet? Please use only Python 3’s standard library so I can run it on my laptop. Inputs are under input/ and outputs should land under build/ so I can zip that folder for the knitting club.

What I need:
1) Build artifacts (required paths):
   - build/top_yarns.csv: Filter, sort, and rank my yarns from input/yarns.csv, selecting the top N from input/config.json (top_n). Ranking rules: sort by rating (descending), then by computed price per 100 yards (ascending), and if still tied, by name (ascending). Exclude any row missing rating or yardage_yards. Include a header with columns exactly in this order: rank, name, brand, fiber, ply, rating, yardage_yards, price_usd, price_per_100yd, notes. Compute price_per_100yd as (price_usd / yardage_yards * 100) rounded to 2 decimals.
   - build/index.html: A single self-contained static page with the site title from input/config.json shown prominently and a table listing the same top N yarns and the same columns as in build/top_yarns.csv. It’s fine to keep the HTML simple; no external assets or network calls.
   - build/deploy_status.md: A short status summary I can paste to our club thread. Include: the current timestamp, the Python version, the input file names and row counts, how many rows were excluded and why (e.g., missing rating or yardage), the value of top_n used, and a bulleted list of the final top N yarns showing name, rating, and price_per_100yd. End with a brief 2–3 sentence summary in a warm Southern grandmother tone about what was “deployed” to build/ and what to expect next.
   - build/club_memo_rewritten.txt: Take input/messages/club_memo.txt and rewrite it for warmth, clarity, and brevity (3–5 sentences). The rewritten memo must (a) mention the site title from input/config.json, (b) reference both build/index.html and build/top_yarns.csv so folks know where to click, and (c) include a friendly sign‑off on the last line starting with a dash (e.g., “- Mae”).

2) Configuration: Read input/config.json for site_title and top_n (use those values, don’t hardcode). All processing should be reproducible: if I delete build/ and re-run your script, I should get the same results from the same inputs.

3) How to run: Provide a single Python script at scripts/deploy.py that creates/overwrites the build/ artifacts exactly as specified. I’ll run it from the repo root with `python3 scripts/deploy.py`.

Notes from Nana: I’ve got strong feelings about yarn quality, so please make that ranking precise and spell out any exclusions in the status. Keep everything local and simple—no external packages or online calls. Thank you, sugar! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
